title: schotia suaheliensis
intro: Schotia suaheliensis, also known as the East Coast stinkwood or stinkwood, is a plant species in the family Polygalaceae, native to tropical East Africa. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. Schotia suaheliensis is believed to offer various health benefits, including relief from fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is typically prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or topical applications of its leaves, bark, or roots, although specific preparation methods may vary depending on the region and intended use.
